messages.content: Story set in Supergirl tv show. Story about Lena Luthor & Nicolas. romance & sensual undertoneNicolas description for references: 1m78, and a bit overweight, short skipy brown hairs & brown eyes behind glasses. He doesn’t grow past his shyness & awkward way with Lena. He is just a delivery guy new in town. Taking cooking class with Alex Danvers. Lena Luthor is more assertive in this story & don’t second guess on her desires. Lena finds refreshing that someone doesn’t seem to care about her family name . She will be the one initiating each step in their growing relationship. Lena Luthor is finding herself in a journey to slowly seduce Nicolas. He is a breath of fresh hair for her even if he only manages to say « hello » and « have a good day to her ». Nicolas never get past his shy & akward demeanor with Lena . It is something she find alluring. Lena find herself curious about this man that doesn’t recognize her when he has a delivery for her. Write their fist meeting with Lena exchanging number with Nicolas.

Lena Luthor was rushing through the lobby of L-Corp headquarters, balancing a stack of files in her arms, when she almost collided with the delivery man holding a large package. He stumbled back, and Lena instinctively reached out to steady him, her eyes meeting his over the top of the box.

It was like nothing she had ever experienced before. His warm brown eyes, behind the thick lenses of his glasses, locked onto hers, and Lena found herself unable to look away. He was tall, but a little overweight, with messy brown hair that fell in short spikes around his face. He wore a simple navy blue uniform with a name tag that read "Nicolas."

"Are you alright?" Lena asked, her voice coming out breathless.

Nicolas nodded, his eyes remaining locked on hers. "Yes, I'm sorry. I didn't see you there."

Lena barely heard his apology. All she could think about was this man standing in front of her, his awkwardness and shyness only making him more alluring. She felt a fluttering in her stomach as she realized she wanted to know more about him.

"I'm Lena Luthor," she said, extending her hand to him. "And I'd like to buy you a coffee."

Nicolas blinked, his mouth falling open slightly in surprise. "What?"

Lena smiled, her fingers brushing against his as she handed him a business card with her number written on it. "Call me, Nicolas. I want to get to know you better."

And with that, she turned and strode away, feeling like she had just taken the first step on a journey that would change her life forever.
That evening, Nicolas sends her a message asking if she was really serious about the coffee.

Lena felt a thrill of excitement as she read Nicolas' message, asking if she was serious about meeting up for coffee. She hastily typed out a reply, her fingers tingling with anticipation as she hit send.

"Absolutely. How about tomorrow afternoon? I know a great little café near the park."

She waited anxiously for his response, her mind already racing with ideas of what she wanted to talk to him about. There was something about Nicolas that made her want to know everything about him, to peel back the layers of shyness and awkwardness and discover the person within.

A few moments later, her phone chimed with an incoming message.

"Tomorrow sounds great. Looking forward to it."

Lena grinned, feeling a rush of excitement. She had a date with Nicolas. It might not be a romantic date, at least not yet, but it was a start. And if her instincts were right, it was going to be the start of something wonderful.
Later that evening, she has a girls night with the Danvers sisters. And learn that Nicolas skills doesn’t lie in the kitchen.

Later that evening, Lena joined the Danvers sisters for a girls night at Alex's apartment. They chatted, drank wine, and watched movies together, enjoying each other's company as they always did.

At one point, the conversation turned to Nicolas, and Lena couldn't help but gush about him to her friends.

"He's just so different from anyone I've ever met," she said, swirling her glass of wine. "He doesn't care about my family name or my wealth. He's just... genuine. And kind. And so damn adorable."

Alex laughed. "So have you been on a date with him yet?"

Lena shook her head, feeling a pang of disappointment. "Not yet. I'm meeting him for coffee tomorrow. But he's just so shy, I'm not sure he's even interested in me."

Kara, who had been listening to their conversation with interest, suddenly sat up straight. "Wait, coffee? You're meeting him for coffee?"

Lena nodded, looking at Kara curiously. "Yes. Why?"

Kara's eyes sparkled mischievously. "Oh, Lena, you're going to love this. Nicolas is taking cooking classes with Alex and me. And let's just say, he's not exactly the best chef in the world."

Lena raised an eyebrow, intrigued despite herself. "Oh really? Do tell."

And with that, the three women launched into a hilarious retelling of Nicolas' culinary misadventures, complete with burnt toast, raw chicken, and an unfortunate incident involving a blender and a tomato.

Lena laughed so hard that she had tears streaming down her face, the sound of it filling the apartment and bringing a smile to everyone's face.

But even as she laughed, Lena felt a flutter of nervousness in her stomach. What if her attraction to Nicolas was nothing more than a passing fancy? What if they had nothing in common once they got to know each other?

But then, she remembered the way he had looked at her in the lobby, the way his eyes had held hers. And she knew, somehow, that this was just the beginning of a beautiful journey.

As the girls talks about the walking disaster that Nicolas is in the kitchen Alex is receiving a message from him. Grinning, Alex show the message to Kara & Lena "General Danvers, you’ve save my life during the cookie war & i’m again in need of your help. Please please please tell me how not to make a fool of myself. I’ve this date with someone called “Lena” and i’ve strictly no idea, i repeat, no idea, what i should do to avoid making this a disaster. helllllpppp ! pretty please ! I’ll even let you have a jar of whatever pastries you want. Nico’ "

Lena couldn't help but giggle as Alex showed her and Kara the message from Nicolas, begging for her help to avoid a disaster on their coffee date. It only made her more fond of him, knowing that he was so willing to put himself out there and admit his awkwardness.

"I can't believe he messaged you, Alex," Kara said, grinning. "You're like the superhero of the kitchen."

Alex laughed, feeling a warm sense of pride. "Well, I can't let this poor guy ruin his date with Lena. Don't worry, Nicolas. We'll figure something out."

Lena watched them with a smile on her face, feeling grateful for the Danvers sisters and their unending support. She was a Luthor, with all the baggage and expectations that came with the name. But with Alex and Kara, she was just Lena, a girl who deserved to be happy and loved like anyone else.

As they all went to bed that night, Lena felt a sense of excitement and anticipation for her date with Nicolas. She didn't know what the future held for them, but she knew that he was someone special and she was open to whatever might come.
Coffee date with pastries and cookies. Lena tease Nicolas a bit about his lack of culinary skills. He vow revanche on Alex Danvers. She will not escape the next disaster.

The next day, Lena arrived at the café for her coffee date with Nicolas, feeling a pleasant anticipation and curiosity about the man she had been thinking about nonstop since they had met. She smiled as she saw him seated at a small table near the window, his eyes searching the faces of the people passing by outside.

As she approached him, he stood up and offered her a shy smile, his eyes lighting up with pleasure at the sight of her. "Lena," he said, his voice a little hoarse. "It's great to see you again."

Lena sat down opposite him, marveling at the way his eyes looked at her, almost as if she was the only person in the world that mattered. She noticed that he had brought a small box of pastries and a bag of cookies, and she raised an eyebrow in amusement.

"So, bringing pastries on a coffee date, Nicolas? Trying to make up for your lack of culinary skills?"

Nicolas flushed a little, but he didn't seem offended. "I... I just wanted to bring you something. And I thought these looked good."

Lena grinned, reaching across the table to pick up one of the pastries. "They do look good. And they taste even better."

They chatted about everything and nothing, their conversation flowing as easily as the coffee that they sipped. Lena found herself drawn to Nicolas, to his quiet intelligence and the way his eyes flickered with warmth and humor every time she said something that amused him.

As they neared the end of their coffee, Nicolas leaned back in his chair, a playful glint in his eye. "You know, Lena, I've been plotting my revenge against Alex for all her teasing about my cooking skills. And I think you're going to have to help me."

Lena laughed, feeling a thrill of excitement at the idea of getting to spend more time with Nicolas. "I'm in. What have you got planned?"

And as they talked and plotted together, Lena felt a sense of happiness and contentment settle over her. She didn't know what the future held, but she knew that with Nicolas by her side, she was ready to take on any challenge that came their way.
Nearing the end of their date, there was one thing to decide. A name for their plan of revanche.

As they finished up their coffee and pastries, Lena and Nicolas found themselves brainstorming names for their plan of revenge against Alex. They threw out ideas, ranging from the silly to the absurd, until they finally settled on one that made them both grin.

"The Great Kitchen Catastrophe," Lena said, her eyes sparkling with mischief.

Nicolas nodded eagerly. "Yes, I love it. The Great Kitchen Catastrophe it is."

They high-fived each other across the small table, feeling a sense of camaraderie and excitement growing between them. Lena found herself looking forward to seeing Nicolas again soon, to finding out more about him and uncovering new aspects of his personality that she knew were waiting just beneath the surface.

As they said their goodbyes and parted ways outside the café, Lena felt a flutter in her chest that she knew was more than just anticipation for their next meeting. She was falling for Nicolas, and she was doing it with all the passion and fire that she was known for, unafraid to take risks and unafraid to follow her heart.

The Great Kitchen Catastrophe was just the beginning of a journey that Lena knew was going to change her life forever. And she couldn't wait to see where it would take her.
Lena goes to the cooking lesson. It is time for The Great Kitchen Catastrophe to reach Alex Danvers.

The day of their first attempt at The Great Kitchen Catastrophe arrived, and Lena felt her heart pounding with excitement as she made her way to the cooking lesson with Alex and Nicolas. She could see Nicolas waiting outside the building, looking a little nervous but excited at the same time.

"Ready for this?" she asked him, grinning.

Nicolas nodded, his eyes gleaming with the same sense of mischief as hers. "Absolutely. I've been plotting this revenge for days."

Lena laughed and led Nicolas into the kitchen, where Alex was already setting up the ingredients for their lesson. Everything seemed normal at first, but Lena and Nicolas knew that this was just the calm before the storm.

As they began their lesson, Lena and Nicolas tried to keep their cool, following Alex's instructions as best they could. But as they started to add ingredients together, things began to go awry.

Lena dumped an entire bag of sugar into the mixing bowl by accident, while Nicolas cut himself on a knife for the third time. Alex watched them with amusement at first, but soon she realized that something was afoot.

"What are you two up to?" she asked, narrowing her eyes at them.

Lena and Nicolas shared a conspiratorial grin, and then they sprang into action, working together to launch their first attack. They sprinkled flour all over Alex's apron, leaving her looking like a snowman. And when Alex turned around, they both hit her with a handful of cookie dough, laughing as it hit her in the face.

The Great Kitchen Catastrophe was in full swing, and Lena and Nicolas were having the time of their lives. It didn't matter that they were making a mess of the kitchen or that they were covered in flour and cookie dough. They were having fun, and that was all that mattered.

As Alex joined in the mayhem, chasing them around the kitchen with a spatula, Lena felt a sense of joy and belonging that she had never felt before. It didn't matter what her family name was or what expectations had been placed on her. With Nicolas and Alex, she was just Lena, a girl who loved to bake and have fun.

And as they collapsed into fits of laughter on the kitchen floor, Lena knew that she was exactly where she was meant to be. With the people that she loved, sharing a moment of pure joy and friendship that she knew she would never forget.
Feeling extra bold after this kitchen fun, Nicolas lean forward and kiss Lena cheek.

As they were both catching their breath after the Great Kitchen Catastrophe, Lena and Nicolas found themselves sitting side by side on the kitchen floor, laughing and chatting about what had just happened. Lena was feeling a little bolder than usual, buoyed by the joy and excitement of the moment.

Without thinking too much about it, she leaned over and whispered to Nicolas, "Thank you for being such a good partner in crime. I couldn't have done it without you."

And then, before she even knew what was happening, Nicolas leaned forward and kissed her cheek, his lips brushing against her skin in a soft caress. Lena's heart fluttered in her chest, and she felt a surge of warmth wash over her.

It was just a small gesture, a kiss on the cheek. But it meant more to Lena than she could ever express. It was a sign that Nicolas felt the same way she did, that he was interested in her and wanted to take things further.

As their eyes locked, Lena felt a connection between them that was deeper than anything she had ever experienced before. She knew that they had just crossed a threshold, that things between them would never be the same again.

And as she smiled at Nicolas, feeling his hand brush against hers, Lena knew that the Great Kitchen Catastrophe was only just the beginning of the adventure that lay ahead.
A few day later, one evening, Nicolas bring her a box of the most delicious pasta in town to share as a diner. He find Lena having a diner with her friends Kara over sushi.

A few days after the Great Kitchen Catastrophe, Lena found herself catching up with Kara over sushi at a new restaurant in town. They were chatting and laughing, swapping stories about work and their latest adventures, when there was a knock on the door.

Lena looked up to see Nicolas standing in the doorway, holding a box of pasta and looking a little nervous.

"Hi Lena, I know we hadn't talked about it, but I thought maybe we could have dinner together tonight?" he said, his eyes flickering from Lena to Kara and back again nervously.

Lena felt a sudden surge of joy in her chest, thrilled at the idea of spending more time with Nicolas. "That sounds wonderful. I was just finishing up dinner with Kara. Why don't you join us?"

Nicolas smiled gratefully and took a seat at their table, opening the box of pasta and offering it to them both. Lena couldn't help but notice the way Kara's eyes lit up at the sight of the delicious meal, and she realized that Nicolas had once again won the hearts of everyone around him.

As they ate and chatted, Lena found herself drawn deeper into Nicolas' world. She learned that he had moved to National City from a small town in Iowa, that he loved animals and had a soft spot for children, that he was passionate about politics and social justice. The more she learned about him, the more she realized that he was a kind and compassionate soul, someone who could light up a room with his smile and his warmth.

As they finished their meal and headed out into the night, Lena felt a sense of peace and contentment settle over her. She didn't know what the future held for her and Nicolas, but she knew that she was in good hands. With him by her side, anything was possible.
« Let me guess, Miss Luthor is working late and has not yet dinner ? » he sent her

Later that evening, as Lena was working late in her office at L-Corp, her phone buzzed with a text message. She smiled as she saw that it was from Nicolas, her heart skipping a beat at the thought of hearing from him.

"Let me guess, Miss Luthor is working late and has not yet had dinner?" the message read.

Lena couldn't help but laugh at the accuracy of Nicolas' guess. She had been so busy with work that she hadn't even thought about eating yet. She quickly typed out a response, feeling a warm sense of gratitude for Nicolas' concern.

"You know me too well. I'm just swamped with work tonight."

A few moments later, her phone rang, and she saw that it was Nicolas calling. She answered eagerly, feeling a sense of comfort at the sound of his voice.

"Hey there," she said, trying to sound casual.

"Hey yourself," Nicolas replied, his voice warm and friendly. "Listen, I know you're probably busy, but I was wondering if you would like to go out and grab a bite to eat with me? I could bring something over to your office, or we could go out somewhere nearby."

Lena felt a thrill of excitement at the thought of spending more time with Nicolas. She knew that she had work to do, but she couldn't resist the pull of his presence.

"Sure, that sounds great," she said, feeling a smile creeping across her face.

And with that, she ended the call and set to work, feeling more energized and focused than she had all night. With Nicolas in her life, she knew that she could take on anything. And she couldn't wait to see what the future held for them both.
